campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council reviewed how the city's money was invested and spent during July 2025, checking the monthly financial reports. They also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, including how people can get around by different methods like transit, biking, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment transactions and financial performance
Monthly treasurer's report showing city finances
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan - improving transit, biking, and walking options

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward with introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and decided to skip the usual first formal reading to speed up the process. The ordinance is scheduled for a final vote at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived
Ordinance set for adoption vote at next regular meeting
Streamlined approval process being used

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from March and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was mostly administrative, focusing on cleaning up paperwork from previous meetings and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six March meetings (including closed sessions and special study sessions)
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc. (specific project details not provided in agenda)
Routine administrative housekeeping from prior town council meetings

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review programs and services for young people in the city. They discussed updates on youth services, summer safety programs, youth empowerment initiatives, and also reviewed the city's code enforcement efforts and library facilities plans.
keyTopics: Youth Services Master Plan - progress update on city programs for children and teens
Safe Summer Initiative Grant - summer safety and activity programs for young people
Youth Intervention Services - annual report on programs helping at-risk youth
Code Enforcement Annual Report - enforcement of city rules and regulations
Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan - updates on library improvements and services

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, that included both in-person and online participation options. Residents were able to join via Zoom or phone to watch and provide public comments on agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants
Meeting conducted as hybrid in-person and online event

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held their regular meeting on April 9, 2026, starting at 7 P.M. with a flag salute ceremony. Three commissioners shared personal stories about their connections to Sunnyvale, and the group approved the official meeting notes from their previous gathering in March.
keyTopics: Human Relations Commission monthly meeting
Commissioner personal stories about Sunnyvale
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Flag salute ceremony

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community initiatives including a presentation about Budget and Bites (likely a food/nutrition program), hear from a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and learn about summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved meeting minutes from their previous session in March.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
Summer jobs available for young residents
Public comment period on non-agenda items
Approval of March 11 meeting minutes

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using both in-person and online options so residents could participate however works best for them. The council is making it easy for people to watch and submit comments about agenda items either through Zoom video conference or by phone.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format - attend in person or join remotely via Zoom
Public comment submission through eComments tab on City Council Agenda page
Phone participation option available (1-669-900-6833)
Remote attendance continues as standard practice for Council meetings

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several planning and procedural items. Key business included updating the city's five-year housing and community development plan, reviewing requests from council members for new projects, and changing rules about how council vacancies are filled.
keyTopics: Updated the city's 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
Reviewed new project requests from individual council members
Changed the council vacancy ordinance (fewer details provided in agenda)
Approved previous meeting minutes and confirmed upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council reviewed and approved the city's natural gas utility budget and rates for the next fiscal year (2027). The council also confirmed the minutes from a previous utilities meeting and adjusted residential and commercial gas service rates.
keyTopics: Natural gas utility budget for fiscal year 2027
Gas rate changes for residential customers (G-1 service)
Gas rate changes for commercial and large business customers (G-2 and G-3 services)
Transfer of utility reserves and general fund payments
